M-066.NAME: vpn-config-download-mime-hardening
M-066.path: backend/app/vpn/router.py frontend/src/pages/Config.tsx frontend/src/lib/api.ts backend/tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py scripts/phase48-config-download-mime-smoke.mjs scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs docs/plans/Phase-48.xml docs/plans/Phase-70.xml docs/plans/Phase-73.xml
M-066.owns: HTTP response semantics and frontend download Blob semantics for WireGuard and AmneziaVPN .conf files.
M-066.purpose: Prevent Android, iOS, and desktop browsers from renaming VPN configuration downloads from .conf to .conf.txt by forcing safe attachment and MIME headers.

exports.fn-build-config-download-response: Return config bytes with attachment, application/octet-stream, filename, filename-star, nosniff, and private no-store headers.
exports.fn-sanitize-config-download-filename: Build ASCII-safe .conf filenames without path separators, duplicate extensions, Unicode surprises, or .txt suffixes.
exports.fn-download-vpn-config: Preserve the authenticated /api/v1/vpn/config/download contract while changing only download response headers and body encoding.
exports.ui-download-config-blob: Create browser download Blobs with application/octet-stream and a .conf download attribute for cached or managed device configs.
exports.fn-download-device-config: Apply the same attachment, filename, nosniff, and no-store guarantees to per-device config downloads.

constraints.constraint-1: Do not change install.sh, deploy scripts, nginx, docker-compose, TLS, VPN profile generation, AWG/WG key material, or existing config text content in Phase-48.
constraints.constraint-2: The download endpoint must stay authenticated and must not expose configs through public URLs.
constraints.constraint-3: Content-Type must not be text/plain for attachment downloads because Android Chrome may append .txt.
constraints.constraint-4: Filename must be ASCII-safe, deterministic, and end with exactly .conf.
constraints.constraint-5: QR, copy-to-clipboard, raw config display, and JSON config retrieval must keep their current behavior unless explicitly changed in a later phase.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: /api/v1/vpn/config/download returns application/octet-stream, attachment, filename .conf, filename-star .conf, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, and Cache-Control no-store/private.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: Response body remains byte-for-byte equivalent to the generated VPN config text after UTF-8 encoding.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Frontend-created download Blobs use application/octet-stream and keep download filenames ending in .conf.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: No server or frontend path emits .conf.txt, text/plain, or unsafe path-derived filenames for config downloads.
acceptance-metrics.metric-5: Backend router tests, frontend build, Phase-48 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and deploy/install diff guard pass.

verification-design.evidence-level-1: Module-local backend tests verify authenticated download headers, safe filename sanitation, exact .conf suffix, body preservation, and pending-trial first-config compatibility.
verification-design.evidence-level-2: Wave-level frontend build and static smoke verify application/octet-stream Blob construction, .conf download attributes, no .conf.txt fallback, and preserved QR/copy/raw config surfaces.
verification-design.evidence-level-3: Phase-level XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose diff guard verify metadata consistency and operational isolation.

execution-evidence.evidence-1: backend/app/vpn/router.py now returns .conf downloads through build_config_download_response with application/octet-stream, attachment filename, filename-star, nosniff, and no-store/private headers.
execution-evidence.evidence-2: frontend/src/pages/Config.tsx and frontend/src/lib/api.ts now use CONFIG_DOWNLOAD_MIME_TYPE application/octet-stream for backend and locally-created config Blob downloads.
execution-evidence.evidence-3: backend/tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py passed 10/10 for route compatibility, mobile-safe download headers, filename sanitation, and auth preservation.
execution-evidence.evidence-4: scripts/phase48-config-download-mime-smoke.mjs passed and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose diff guard was clean.

phase-70-execution-evidence.evidence-1: Phase-70 kept /api/v1/vpn/config/download byte-for-byte payload and application/octet-stream attachment headers unchanged while tuning only QR rendering parameters.

phase-71-executed-scope.primary-behaviors: Per-device .conf download must reuse existing application/octet-stream attachment semantics and must never fall back to text/plain or .conf.txt filenames.
phase-71-executed-scope.verification: Phase-71 backend tests and smoke compare the selected-device config payload with the per-device download body without printing config text or private keys.

M-066.hotspot: H-053: Mobile browsers may rename .conf downloads to .txt unless the backend and frontend force attachment, octet-stream, filename, and nosniff semantics
M-066.phase-71-evidence: Backend tests and Phase-71 smoke passed for selected-device .conf download using octet-stream attachment headers and selected-device payload parity without printing config material.

phase-73-executed-scope.primary-behaviors: AmneziaVPN user flow explicitly points to the selected-device .conf download/import path while preserving application/octet-stream Blob and backend attachment semantics.
phase-73-executed-scope.verification: Phase-48 MIME smoke remains the regression gate for headers; Phase-73 smoke verifies AmneziaVPN .conf guidance and protected backend/download-surface guard.

M-066.phase-73-evidence: Phase-73 smoke passed for AmneziaVPN .conf guidance and confirmed download MIME code path markers remain present; download endpoints were not changed.
